meta_title: For Students: debugging with AI to Solve stuck with basic apps (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: Debugging with AI: Advanced Checklist for Student Builders
content.seo_description: Break through your coding plateau. Master AI-assisted debugging with Antigravity and Claude Code to solve complex technical blockers in your projects.
content.answer_block: Stop staring at cryptic stack traces. By integrating Antigravity for architectural analysis and Claude Code for iterative CLI debugging, you shift from passive reading to active, AI-augmented problem solving. This workflow transforms your IDE into a senior-level pairing session, slashing resolution times and leveling up your engineering intuition.
content.intro:
You are staring at a screen that feels more like a prison than a canvas. The terminal is bleeding red errors, your logic is tangled in a web of spaghetti code, and the gap between your ambition and your output feels like an unbridgeable canyon. You have built the basics, but now the walls are closing in, and every line of code feels like a gamble.

This isn't a failure of intelligence; it’s a failure of tooling. Professional engineering isn't about knowing every syntax rule; it’s about mastering the feedback loop. By leveraging high-context AI agents, you can stop guessing and start architecting. It is time to treat your IDE not as a text editor, but as a collaborative engine.

steps.title: Isolate the Architectural Failure
steps.description: Before fixing code, define the boundaries of the bug. Use AI to map the dependency graph so you understand where the silent failure originates.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current project structure and identify which modules are likely causing a state-synchronization error based on these logs: [Insert Logs].
steps.claude_code_command: claude --read-only --ask 'Map the dependency flow for the auth-service module'.

steps.title: Contextual Error Simulation
steps.description: Feed the stack trace and the surrounding environment configuration to Claude to generate a localized hypothesis.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Simulate the execution path of the current function. Where does the type mismatch occur in the data transformation layer?
steps.claude_code_command: claude --ask 'Analyze this stack trace and provide three potential root causes'.

steps.title: Iterative Patch Generation
steps.description: Generate a targeted fix that adheres to your existing project design patterns rather than introducing new technical debt.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the faulty component to handle null values gracefully without breaking the existing interface contracts.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --apply 'Refactor the data transformation function to include robust error handling'.

steps.title: Automated Regression Validation
steps.description: Use AI to write the unit test that confirms the fix works and prevents future regressions in that specific logic block.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Jest test suite that triggers the previously identified edge case to ensure the fix holds under pressure.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --ask 'Generate a comprehensive unit test for the newly patched module'.

steps.title: Post-Mortem Documentation
steps.description: Force the AI to explain the 'why' behind the bug so you learn the underlying system architecture, preventing the same mistake in your next project.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Explain the technical rationale for this bug in simple terms. What design pattern should I have used to avoid this?
steps.claude_code_command: claude --ask 'Explain the architectural flaw that caused this bug and how to prevent it in the future'.

pro_tips: Always provide the AI with your 'system prompt' or coding style guide file to ensure the generated code matches your project's specific conventions.
When debugging, feed the AI the 'package.json' or 'go.mod' file first so it understands your environment constraints before analyzing logic.
If Claude Code is stuck, use the '--verbose' flag to see exactly which files it is indexing for its decision-making process.
Treat your AI agent like a junior developer: give it specific constraints (e.g., 'no external libraries') to force creative, high-quality solutions.
Before finalizing a fix, ask the AI to perform a 'security audit' on the patch to catch potential vulnerabilities you might have missed.
content.next_steps: Once you have successfully debugged your current project, document the 'bug-fix' workflow in your portfolio. Understanding how to use AI to resolve complex errors is a highly sought-after skill for junior roles.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer a student struggling with errors; you are an engineer mastering the craft of AI-augmented development. Stop fighting your code and start commanding it. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
